# Three Baryon Interaction Generated by Determinant Interaction of Quarks

> We discuss the three-baryon interaction generated by the determinant interaction of quarks, known as the Kobayashi-Maskawa-'t Hooft (KMT) interaction. The expectation value of the KMT interaction operator is calculated in fully-antisymmetrized quark-cluster model wave functions for one-, two- and three-octet baryon states. The three-baryon potential from the KMT interaction is found to be repulsive for $NNΛ$ and $NΛΛ$ systems, while it is zero for the $NNN$ system. The strength and range of the three-baryon potential are found to be comparable to those for the $NNN$ three-body potential obtained in lattice QCD simulations. The contribution to the $Λ$ single particle potential in nuclear matter is found to be 0.28 MeV and 0.73 MeV in neutron matter and symmetric nuclear matter at normal nuclear density, respectively. These repulsive forces are not enough to solve the hyperon puzzle, but may be measured in high-precision hyperisotope experiments.

## Key claim

The three-baryon interaction generated by the Kobayashi-Maskawa-'t Hooft (KMT) determinant interaction of quarks is repulsive for \(NN\Lambda\) and \(N\Lambda\Lambda\) systems but zero for the \(NNN\) system; however, the resulting repulsive forces are not strong enough to solve the hyperon puzzle in neutron stars, though they may be detectable in high-precision hyperisotope experiments.

## Method

The authors calculated the expectation value of the KMT interaction operator in fully-antisymmetrized quark-cluster model wave functions for one-, two-, and three-octet baryon states, using non-relativistic \((0s)^3\) harmonic oscillator wave functions for quarks in baryons. The KMT interaction was treated in its non-relativistic form as a zero-range three-body (six-fermi) interaction among quarks, and the matrix elements were evaluated for configurations where two or three baryons are located at the same spatial point. Color factors and flavor-spin-spatial factors were computed for all quark exchange diagrams in the three-baryon systems.

## Key numbers

- The contribution to the \(\Lambda\) single particle potential in nuclear matter is 0.28 MeV in neutron matter and 0.73 MeV in symmetric nuclear matter at normal nuclear density (\(\rho_0 \simeq 0.16~\mathrm{fm}^{-3}\)).
- The KMT interaction strength parameter \(\gD\) is fixed by meson properties, with values \(\gD\Lambda^5 = -9.29\) (Hatsuda 1994) or \(\gD\Lambda^5 = -12.36\) (Rehberg 1995) in the Nambu-Jona-Lasinio model.
- Color factors for three-baryon quark exchanges take values \(\pm 1\), \(\pm 1/3\), \(\pm 1/9\), and 0 depending on the exchange type (direct, baryon exchange, one-quark exchange, two-quark exchange, cyclic three-quark exchange).

## Context

This work extends the study of three-baryon forces from a quark-level perspective, complementing phenomenological approaches such as the multi-pomeron exchange model (Yamamoto 2014) and the auxiliary field diffusion Monte-Carlo calculations with \(\Lambda NN\) forces (Lonardoni 2013, 2014). The KMT interaction, originally introduced to account for the axial \(U(1)\) anomaly (Kobayashi 1970, 1971; 't Hooft 1976), provides a microscopic origin for repulsive three-baryon forces involving hyperons. The results enable future comparisons with lattice QCD simulations of three-nucleon forces (HAL QCD method, Doi 2011) and may guide experimental searches for three-baryon effects in hypernuclei.
